title: The Peak That Was Never There

features.name: the-summit-that-keeps-rising
features.desc-on-touch: A tower of ridges, each one crowned with mist. You reach the top and discover it was never the top. The mountain learns as it climbs.

features.name: the-unmarked-boundary
features.desc-on-touch: A burl rendering the crown of an ancient tree, but the tree-top opens onto cloud instead of sky. No peak. Just the ascent continuing.

features.name: the-cloud-pool-gathering
features.desc-on-touch: Water that gathers where the peak should be. Clouds are pouring into it. The container is filling with sky.

features.name: the-false-summit-marker
features.desc-on-touch: A glyph marking the mountain's peak—carved upside-down, so readers can't tell if they're at the top or still ascending.

features.name: the-staircase-to-nowhere
features.desc-on-touch: Steps carved in wireframe, visible but not solid. Climbers have discovered they're walking on the mountain's intention, not its substance.

inscription: the ridge climbs and climbs; the summit keeps ascending; at its throat, up becomes indistinguishable from down; the map here has learned to unmap itself—the only true peak is the one you never reach.

file.name: Unmapped_Ridge_Climbing_Log_Fragment.txt
file.note: notes from climbers who reached the summit and found only sky and the echoing sound of further climbing

exits.label: back down to the approach where height still meant something
deeper into cloud, where the mountain learns it was never stone
into the canopy where peaks learn to fall as leaves
